items.area: Champion / internal advocate at Grupo Argos
items.source: Next_Step_History__c review, Jan-Jun 2026; no champion named anywhere

metrics.narrative: No quantified business problem or value case has ever been articulated by the relevant stakeholder_Business_Problem__c, Business_Objectives__c and Value_to_Customer__c are all blank. The only pain signal on record is second-hand via the partner: on 3 Mar 2026 Argos was reported to be 'looking for more information on what a POC would look like' for spend analysis, which shows interest but no quantified metric or cost of inaction.

champion.narrative: No individual champion has been identified at Grupo Argos or within the EY partner team as an internal advocate. The relationship runs entirely through the EY partner organization, which has gone from actively relaying pricing/business-case material (through 26 May 2026) to reporting 'no update' (16 Jun 2026) to citing external blockers, a leadership change and a national election, as reasons progress has slowed (29 Jun 2026). That shift raises the possibility the partner itself lacks a strong advocate inside Argos.

competition.narrative: No named competing vendor is on record (Competitors__c is blank); the standing risk is inertia, i.e. the buyer doing nothing, which prior deal history already flags as the live alternative. That risk is elevated by the reported Grupo Argos leadership change and election-driven slowdown as of 29 Jun 2026 (about 10 days ago), which could deprioritize any S2P transformation budget under new leadership.

identify_pain.narrative: Grupo Argos's own words on the underlying pain have never been captured directly. The only articulated interest is via the partner: wanting to understand what a spend-analysis POC would look like (3 Mar 2026) and requesting SaaS/implementation pricing data (18 Feb 2026), implying interest in spend visibility and S2P transformation, but neither quantifies the pain or names who owns it inside Argos.

paper_process.narrative: The opportunity is still in Formal Evaluation with no commercial agreement in place, so no paper process (MSA/SOW/contracting) has begun and none is referenced anywhere in the record.

economic_buyer.narrative: No economic buyer has ever been named. Executive_Sponsor_Identified__c is false and X10a_Sponsor__c / Who_will_approve_budget__c are blank. Every touch on this deal has run through the EY partner, and zero Grupo Argos contact roles exist on the opportunity, so there is no visibility into who at Argos controls budget or must approve the investment. This gap is compounded by the partner's 29 Jun 2026 report of a leadership change at Argos, which may have displaced whatever sponsor existed before.

decision_process.narrative: A rough sequence is visible even without named decision-makers: Grupo Argos requested implementation/pricing data via the partner (18 Feb 2026), the partner presented updated commercials with four pricing scenarios (24 Apr 2026), and a business case was shared with the partner for the buyer (26 May 2026). No named approver, budget owner, or internal Argos timeline has surfaced, and the process has produced no confirmed output from Argos since the leadership change was reported on 29 Jun 2026.

decision_criteria.narrative: Business_Requirements__c and Shoe_Fit_Criteria_Met__c are both blank/false and no call or note describes a formal evaluation rubric. The closest proxy is Grupo Argos requesting implementation and SaaS-pricing data (18 Feb 2026) and asking what a spend-analysis POC would look like (3 Mar 2026), both relayed via the partner, neither of which is documented criteria.

items.name: Amount increased $100K → $350K
items.summary: Deal value jumped 3.5x in one update, signaling a major scope expansion or corrected budget figure rather than incremental upsell — but no note or activity explains the driver, so the reason behind the revision needs to be confirmed.

day_summary.overall: The Grupo Argos deal took a sharp turn upward, with the deal size more than tripling from $100K to $350K in a single update. That kind of jump usually means either a discovery call uncovered a much bigger use case, more business units got pulled into scope, or the buyer clarified their real budget — but there's no accompanying note explaining which. Worth confirming with the deal owner what drove the revision before it's reflected in forecast conversations.
